address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

msContents.summary: A composite volume in five parts: Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel's covering letter, bound in as additional leaves; six short pieces described as "Writings of Ḥaḍrat-i-Quddús" (Mullá Muḥammad 'Alí of Bárfurúsh, martyred 1849); the Naghamátu'r-Rúḥ ("Songs of the Spirit") by Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel, a continuous Arabic text with his own interspersed commentary; Laálí va Majálí, an imitation and expansion by Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel of the Sententiae of 'Alí ibn Abí Ṭálib (179 sections of aphorisms arranged alphabetically); and a short untitled Arabic piece which, from an internal allusion to the Báb's death, Browne judges was written by Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel rather than the Báb.

author.persName: Janāb-i Quddūs, Ḥājjī Mullā Muḥammad ʻAlī Barfurūshī
حاجي ملا محمد علي جناب قدوس برفروشي

msItem.title: Ās̲ār quddūsīyah
آثار قدوسيه
Writings of Janāb-i Quddūs (six separate pieces)
msItem.note: Browne remarks that these six specimens "sufficiently show that his Arabic is even more open to the grammarian's criticism than is usually the case with Bábí writings in that language."
msItem.incipit: بسمه العلی الکبیر المتکبر الحمد لله الذی قد... (no. 1, f. 1a)

msItem.title: Naghamāt al-rūḥ
نغمات الروح
Songs of the Spirit
msItem.note: Text (in naskh) with interspersed commentary (in nim-shikastah), both composed by Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel. The heading "vol. ii" prefixed to the text implies a first volume Browne did not possess. Cyphers appended at the end (repeated at the end of item 3) indicate, per Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel's own oral statement to Browne, that the writing is his, though he declined to explain their meaning further; Browne speculates they relate to abjad notation.
msItem.incipit: لا اله الا الله بسمه الباقی الملک الدیموم المستعان الحمد لله ممسک السماء بقدرته...
msItem.explicit: ... و قل الله ربی و انه لقوی حفیظ و قل هو الحق و انه لمعی یهدین و الحمد لله رب العالمین (f. 101b)

msItem.title: Laʼālī va majālī
لئالي ومجالي
msItem.note: An imitation and expansion of the well-known Sententiae of 'Alí ibn Abí Ṭálib (first published in Europe, in Arabic with Persian translation and Latin glossary, by Professor Stickel, Jena, 1834). Consists of 179 sections of short, disconnected aphorisms, excluding the preface, arranged in groups alphabetically by the initial letter of each aphorism.
msItem.incipit: لا اله الا الله لئالی و مجالی بسم الله الکافی المنّان الحمد لله الاول حیث لا اول قبله...

msItem.title: Short untitled piece
msItem.note: Written in naskh; separated from the preceding item by three blank pages. Browne judges, from an allusion to the Báb's death, that this piece is "certainly not by the Báb."
msItem.incipit: قد ضلت الکل کلها وما عرفوا مقدار حق...
msItem.explicit: ... وما قدر الله للذين کفروا الا نار جهنم حسبهم یردونها وانهم کانوا فیها من المخلدین (f. 208b)

supportDesc.extent: Foliated α, β, and 1-208 (numerous leaves blank throughout, per Browne) 20.5 12.75

layoutDesc.layout: 14 lines to the page in the first and last parts (naskh); 17 in the second and third parts (nim-shikastah, which Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel called "shikastah-i-ḥayván").

handDesc.handNote: Parts I and V (the Quddús writings and the untitled piece): naskh.
Parts II and III (the Naghamátu'r-Rúḥ and Laálí va Majálí): nim-shikastah ("shikastah-i-ḥayván").

history.provenance: Sent with a covering letter (bound into the manuscript as additional ff. α-β) dated Jumádá'th-Thání 18th, AH 1307 (4 February 1890), by Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel from Famagusta, Cyprus.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: "Manuscript description based on. R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932. "; provenance and item notes expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábī Manuscripts," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Jul. 1892, pp. 483-492.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBF_4.xml) into this Fihrist record: summary, item notes, incipit/explicit samples, physical description, hand notes, and provenance history rewritten from Browne's 1892 JRAS catalogue account (pp. 483-492). The draft's separate "covering letter" item (part I) was folded into provenance rather than kept as a distinct msItem, matching this record's existing 4-item structure. Arabic quotations are unproofed OCR (Tesseract), flagged cert="low". Person/place authority keys reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel, Cyprus, Browne). Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Julian Cook Changed person and work ids (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Adjusted summary using add-summaries2.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
Andrew Morrison Removed origDate tags around something which is not a date using fix-dates.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
